editor_note: The prose itself is fluent, idiomatic, native-sounding Arabic with consistent informal instructional register (أنت/جرّب/تخيّل/قسّمه) and no semantic drift — the intro, variational-principle, workflow, optimizer and conclusion all faithfully track the English. The problem is systematic within-file terminology inconsistency in core domain terms, which fails the bar. "Hamiltonian" is rendered three ways depending on section: هاملتون/مؤثر هاملتون in the Schrödinger intro (L56, L62), هاميلتوني/الهاميلتوني in the VQE-workflow block (L213-221), and هاملتوني/الهاملتوني in the Step-1/2/3 execution sections (L332-340, L579-587, L706-708) — plus mixed plurals هاميلتونيات (L221) / للهاميلتونيين (L1027). "ground state energy" flips between the correct طاقة الحالة الأساسية (most of file) and the calqued طاقة الحالة الأرضية ("earth/floor state", wrong convention) in the H-atom results and check-in sections (L673, L698, L701, L706, L716-718, L819). "ansatz" is kept Latin ansatz/الـ ansatz through the body (L215-367) but switches to the transliteration الأنساتز/أنساتزات in the Conclusion, Review and Appendix (L1006-1084). "variational" alternates between التباديلي (title, L46/L209) and مبدأ التباين (L226, L1006), and "molecule" between جزيء and جزيئة (L877/L999/L1008). Each individual choice is defensible, but none is applied consistently, so a learner meets two or three names for Hamiltonian, ansatz and ground-state within one lesson — that is the ≥3 within-file inconsistencies that define FAIL, even though fluency and meaning are otherwise strong.

examples.source: Hamiltonian operator / molecular Hamiltonian
examples.translation: مؤثر هاملتون (L56); هاميلتوني الجزيء (L213); هاملتوني الكيوبت (L334)
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering of "Hamiltonian" and use it throughout, e.g. الهاملتوني (and plural الهاملتونيات), aligning the intro, workflow and execution sections.
examples.why: A core term is spelled/formed three different ways across sections of the same lesson (هاملتون / هاميلتوني / هاملتوني), which can read as three concepts to a learner.

examples.source: ground state energy
examples.translation: طاقة الحالة الأساسية (L340) vs طاقة الحالة الأرضية (L673, L706)
examples.suggested: طاقة الحالة الأساسية everywhere
examples.why: الأرضية is a literal calque of "ground" (earth/floor); the established physics term is الأساسية, which the file itself uses elsewhere — so the same concept gets two names within the file.

examples.source: ansatz / ansätze
examples.translation: Latin ansatz / الـ ansatz (L215, L367) vs الأنساتز / أنساتزات (L1006, L1063, L1074)
examples.suggested: Choose one convention (keep Latin ansatz, or transliterate الأنساتز) and apply it for the whole document
examples.why: The lesson keeps the term in Latin script through the body, then silently switches to the Arabic transliteration in the Conclusion/Review/Appendix, breaking consistency of the single most-repeated technical term.

examples.source: variational principle
examples.translation: المبدأ التباديلي (L46, L68) vs مبدأ التباين (L226, L1006)
examples.suggested: Use المبدأ التباديلي consistently to match the title and section heading
examples.why: "variational" is rendered التباديلي in the title and headings but التباين in two body passages, splitting the lesson's central concept across two terms.

editor_note: This is well-edited, fluent Arabic technical prose that reads as written by a native quantum educator, not raw MT: the intro on position/momentum uncertainty (line 46-53), the Fourier-series intuition, and the collapse/measurement narrative (line 287-293) all teach with the same clarity and emphasis as the English, and the informal teaching register (تذكّر، لنبدأ، دعنا) holds throughout. Core domain terms are correct and largely consistent: قياس (measurement), كيوبت (qubit), بوابة هادامارد (Hadamard gate), تراكب (superposition), الحالة الذاتية (eigenstate), مشاهَد/مشاهَدات (observable). The issues are isolated within-file inconsistencies, none misleading. (1) "momentum" drifts between الزخم الخطي (line 46, and the Critical Concepts list line 939) and the less-standard كمية التحرك (line 432, 491) for the very same physical quantity. (2) The Qiskit primitive "estimator" is kept in English almost everywhere (estimator, line 434/583/638) but localized once as المُقدِّر (line 682), breaking the otherwise-consistent convention of leaving primitive names in English (Sampler is never localized). (3) "expectation value" alternates between قيمة التوقع (line 389/483/579) and القيمة المتوقعة (line 876) — both acceptable, just inconsistent. (4) Minor gender slip في تلك الاتجاه (line 46; الاتجاه is masculine → should be ذلك). None of these cause semantic drift; the physics, the inverted-measurement-order argument, and all caveats survive intact.

examples.source: momentum along that direction / linear position and momentum
examples.translation: الزخم الخطي في تلك الاتجاه (line 46) ... كمية التحرك على نفس الاتجاه (line 432); الموضع الخطي وكمية التحرك (line 491)
examples.suggested: Use الزخم الخطي consistently (as in line 46 and Critical Concepts line 939) rather than switching to كمية التحرك
examples.why: The same physical quantity (linear momentum) is rendered two ways within one file; الزخم is the standard physics term while كمية التحرك is a less conventional choice, and the switch is jarring to a domain reader.

examples.source: using estimator for many choices of the state vector
examples.translation: باستخدام المُقدِّر لاختيارات كثيرة من متجه الحالة
examples.suggested: باستخدام estimator (keep the primitive name in English, as done in lines 434, 583, 638)
examples.why: Everywhere else the Qiskit primitive name 'estimator' is kept in English (matching Sampler, which is never localized); localizing it once to المُقدِّر breaks the file's own convention and the link between prose and code identifier.

examples.source: find the expectation value of Z / with the expectation value of Y maximized
examples.translation: قيمة التوقع (line 389/483/579) vs القيمة المتوقعة (line 876)
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering of 'expectation value' (قيمة التوقع) and use it throughout
examples.why: Both forms are valid Arabic for expectation value, but alternating them within the same teaching file is an avoidable inconsistency a careful editor would unify.

editor_note: This reads like genuine native Czech technical prose, not machine translation: phrasings like "Nováček v kvantovém výpočetnictví? Začni kurzem.", "Qiskit už znáš? Přejdi rovnou na tutoriál nebo průvodce.", and "uživatelsky přívětivý open-source frontend" are idiomatic and well-edited. Domain terminology is correct and internally consistent: "obvody" (circuits), "provázání" (entanglement), "transpilaci" (transpilation), "zmírnění chyb" (error mitigation), "model šumu" (noise model), with "kernel Jupyter", "backend(y)", "Qiskit", "Bezserverový" (serverless) sensibly kept/rendered in the conventional Czech dev register. The informal "ty" convention holds throughout (ti pomáhá, abys mohl, přejdi, vytvoř si). Two isolated, non-misleading slips keep this from a clean PASS: (1) line 49 narrows the English "quantum basics" to "základů kvantové mechaniky" (quantum *mechanics*), a slight semantic narrowing; (2) "Simulátorový mód" uses the colloquial loanword "mód" where formal Czech tech writing would prefer "režim" — defensible given the informal tone and used consistently, but a register nit. Neither misleads the reader.

examples.source: Structured courses from quantum basics to advanced topics
examples.translation: Strukturované kurzy od základů kvantové mechaniky po pokročilá témata
examples.suggested: Strukturované kurzy od základů kvantové informatiky / od kvantových základů po pokročilá témata
examples.why: English 'quantum basics' is broader than 'quantum mechanics'; the translation narrows it to a specific subfield. Isolated, non-misleading.

examples.source: simulator mode
examples.translation: simulátorovým módem / Simulátorový mód
examples.suggested: simulátorový režim (optional)
examples.why: 'mód' is a colloquial loanword; 'režim' is the more standard Czech technical term. Used consistently and acceptable in this informal register, so only a minor nit.

editor_note: This reads like genuine Czech technical writing, not machine translation: the introductory history ("měli ... mlhavou představu", "propast mezi klasickými a kvantovými počítači ještě prohloubili", "vytěžit co nejvíce informací v jediném běhu je subtilní umění") and the casual asides ("Nezalekni se, pokud kódu úplně nerozumíš") are idiomatic and well-edited. Domain terminology is correct and internally consistent: "hradlo" (gate), "obvod" (circuit), "qubit" kept in English, "superpozice", "interference", "vyvážená/konstantní" (balanced/constant), "černá skříňka" (black box), "skalární součin" (dot product), "provázanost" (entanglement), "transpilovat", and "phase kickback" sensibly left untranslated and used consistently. Meaning is faithful throughout (the "only a polynomial speedup" claim, the run-twice and B-V/D-J-equivalence answers all match the English), and the informal "ty" register with gendered forms like "zjistil(a)" holds consistently. The only defects are two isolated grammar slips, not semantic drift: line 47 "kvantový fyzici" should be the animate-plural "kvantoví fyzici", and line 583 reads "Toto hradlo ... a aplikují funkci" where the singular subject "hradlo" requires the singular "aplikuje" (English "applies") — a number-agreement error carried over awkwardly. Neither misleads the reader; both are the kind of typo a final native proofread would catch.

examples.source: In the early 1980's, quantum physicists and computer scientists had a vague notion
examples.translation: Na počátku 80. let 20. století měli kvantový fyzici a informatici mlhavou představu
examples.suggested: Na počátku 80. let 20. století měli kvantoví fyzici a informatici mlhavou představu
examples.why: Grammatical slip: the nominative plural masculine-animate adjective must end in -í, so 'kvantoví fyzici', not 'kvantový fyzici'. Isolated typo in otherwise native prose.

examples.source: This gate will take the first n qubits as input ... and applies the function f(x)=s·x to the output qubit
examples.translation: Toto hradlo vezme prvních n qubitů jako vstup ... a aplikují funkci f(x)=s · x na výstupní qubit
examples.suggested: Toto hradlo vezme prvních n qubitů jako vstup ... a aplikuje funkci f(x)=s · x na výstupní qubit
examples.why: Number-agreement error: singular subject 'hradlo' takes the singular verb 'aplikuje' (English 'applies'); the plural 'aplikují' is ungrammatical here. Meaning still clear, so a slip rather than true drift.

editor_note: This is native, well-edited German mathematical prose — it reads as if a German-speaking quantum information theorist wrote it, not like machine translation. The connective sentences that carry the proof flow naturally: "Nun kommen wir zu dem Trick, der diesen Teil des Beweises zum Funktionieren bringt", "Es bleibt zu überprüfen, dass die erforderliche Bedingung an A_0,…,A_{N-1} erfüllt ist", and the mathematical imperatives ("Sei die klassische Zustandsmenge … gleich Σ", "Betrachte die ersten n Spalten", "Berechnen wir nun das Skalarprodukt") all hit the correct German proof register. Domain terminology is correct and fully consistent throughout: "Dichtematrizen", "positiv semidefinit", "lineare Abbildung", "Kraus-/Stinespring-/Choi-Darstellung", "Eigenwerte/Eigenvektoren", "die Spur erhalten", "treu" (faithful), "Skalarprodukt" (inner product), "Vektorisierung", "Orthonormalsystem", "Gram-Schmidt-Orthogonalisierungsverfahren", with "Qubits" correctly kept in English. Meaning is faithful with no drift — negations and quantifiers ("muss mN/n keine ganze Zahl sein" for "may not be an integer", "N nie größer als nm") are rendered correctly, and the "garbage system G" is rendered as "Hilfssystem (auch ‚garbage'-System genannt)" which is an accurate and pedagogically helpful gloss rather than a deviation. The informal "wir"/imperative convention holds with no formal-Sie slips. No issues to flag.

This reads as native, well-edited technical Spanish written by someone who knows the circuit-cutting domain, not as machine translation. Domain terminology is correct and fully consistent within the file: "valores esperados" (expectation values), "compuertas" (gates), "observable/subobservable", "autoestado" (eigenstate), "base de Pauli", "base tomográficamente completa", "primitiva Sampler", "posprocesamiento", with "qubit", "backend" and "transpilar" correctly kept in their conventional forms. A nice idiomatic touch is rendering upstream/downstream as "circuito aguas arriba/aguas abajo", which is the natural Spanish technical phrasing. The informal "tú" register holds throughout ("Ten en cuenta", "Observa", "asegúrate", "te resultó interesante"). I checked the load-bearing physics claims for drift — the deterministic 0/1 classical caveat vs. mixed-state qubit (line 31), the "$+1$ for every qubit site irrespective of $\vec{\phi}$" claim (line 76), the with/without-cutting equality (line 78), the "+1 qubit" wire-cut count (line 161), and the upstream-measured / downstream-prepared mapping (line 229) — all are faithful with no inverted conditions or swapped quantities. The only trivially notable spot is line 20 rendering "users should understand" as "deberán ser capaces de" (should be able to), a common and harmless learning-outcomes idiom that does not mislead.

examples.source: the upstream circuit must be measured in a Pauli basis, and the downstream circuit must be prepared in the eigenstate of the basis
examples.translation: el circuito aguas arriba debe medirse en una base de Pauli, y el circuito aguas abajo debe prepararse en el autoestado de esa base
examples.why: Correct, natural domain rendering of upstream/downstream and eigenstate; the measure/prepare roles are mapped without inversion.

examples.source: After going through this tutorial, users should understand:
examples.translation: Tras completar este tutorial, los usuarios deberán ser capaces de:
examples.why: Mild idiomatic shift from 'should understand' to 'should be able to', but it is the conventional Spanish learning-outcomes phrasing and does not change meaning.
examples.suggested: Tras completar este tutorial, los usuarios deberían comprender:

editor_note: This reads as genuine native Hebrew technical prose, not raw MT: the informal register (אתה/תוכל/קבץ/הרץ) is consistent, and the opening abstraction paragraph ("מעביר את יצירת גרסאות ה-Circuit היקרה לצד השרת... מבלי לפגוע בביצועים") flows the way a Hebrew-speaking quantum engineer would actually write it. Core terminology is correct and consistent — "ערכי ציפייה" for expectation values, "מודל רעש"/"למידת מודל רעש והזרקתו" for noise model learning and injection, "שינויי בסיס" for basis changes, "שערים חד- ודו-Qubit" for one-/two-qubit gates — and field-kept English (Sampler, Estimator, primitive, twirling, box, samplex, NoiseLearnerV3) is handled sensibly. Issues are isolated, not misleading: (1) in step 5, "extrapolate mitigated expectation values" is softened to "לחלץ" (extract), losing the extrapolation nuance; (2) in step 4, "execute the circuit variants" becomes "ירוץ" (intransitive "will run") rather than the transitive "יריץ"/"יבצע", plus a tripled-yod spelling slip "שיייצר"; (3) "Because these two constructs..." is rendered "מכיוון שני מבנים אלה" with the conjunction ש dropped (should be "מכיוון ששני"), reading awkwardly; (4) "what bound circuits to run" → "circuits קשורים" is a weak literal calque of "bound" (tied) where parameter-bound is meant. None distort the technical meaning and the page teaches as clearly as the English, so MINOR_ISSUES rather than FAIL.

examples.source: extrapolate mitigated expectation values from the execution results
examples.translation: לחלץ ערכי ציפייה מופחתי-שגיאה (mitigated) מתוצאות ההרצה
examples.suggested: לבצע אקסטרפולציה של ערכי ציפייה מופחתי-שגיאה מתוצאות ההרצה
examples.why: 'לחלץ' means extract; English 'extrapolate' is a specific technique (e.g. zero-noise extrapolation). The rendering flattens the method into mere extraction, a mild semantic softening.

examples.source: the Executor primitive, which will generate and execute the circuit variants as instructed
examples.translation: ה-primitive של Executor, שיייצר וירוץ את גרסאות ה-Circuit בהתאם להוראות
examples.suggested: ...שייצר ויריץ/ויבצע את גרסאות ה-Circuit בהתאם להוראות
examples.why: 'ירוץ' is intransitive ('will run [itself]'); the Executor executes the variants, requiring the transitive 'יריץ'/'יבצע'. Also 'שיייצר' has a spurious tripled yod (should be 'שייצר').

examples.source: Because these two constructs are created on the client side
examples.translation: מכיוון שני מבנים אלה נוצרים בצד הלקוח
examples.suggested: מכיוון ששני מבנים אלה נוצרים בצד הלקוח
examples.why: The subordinating ש is dropped, merging 'מכיוון ש' + 'שני' into 'מכיוון שני' ('because two'), which a native writer would not produce.

examples.source: what parameters to generate and what bound circuits to run
examples.translation: בדיוק אילו פרמטרים לייצר ואילו circuits קשורים להריץ
examples.suggested: ...ואילו circuits מאוגדים/עם פרמטרים קשורים להריץ
examples.why: 'קשורים' (tied/related) is a literal calque of 'bound'; in this primitives context 'bound' means parameter-bound, so the plain 'קשורים' is ambiguous and slightly off-register.

editor_note: Overall this reads as competent Hebrew technical prose with faithful meaning and no semantic drift — claims, quantities, conditions and the benchmark table all match the English. Core domain terminology is correct and used consistently: expectation value = "ערך ציפייה", fidelity = "נאמנות", scalability = "מדרגיות", light cone = "חרוט האור", non-commuting Pauli strings = "מחרוזות Pauli שאינן מתחלפות", amplitude estimation = "אמידת משרעת"; observable/Gate/Circuit/Transpilation/Hamiltonian are kept in English consistently, which is a defensible convention here. The informal masculine register ("תוכל", "אל תהסס", "המחקר שלך") holds throughout. The blemishes are isolated and non-misleading: (1) "ה-Gates הזמינים ילידים" (line 33) is translationese for "natively available gates" — "ילידים" is a wrong-register literalism a native writer would replace with "הזמינים באופן מקורי/טבעי"; (2) "Gates $Rzz(\theta)$ אופטמיזציה על ידי Qedma" (line 40) drops the participle and reads as broken syntax for "Qedma-optimized gates" (should be "שעברו אופטימיזציה"); (3) "אופטימיזציה" is spelled inconsistently — clipped "אופטמיזציה"/"שאופטמו"/"אופטמו" (lines 33, 40) vs the standard "אופטימיזציה" (lines 41, 66); (4) gender-agreement slip "שלושה לולאות" (line 62) where feminine "לולאה" requires "שלוש". None of these change meaning or mislead the reader.

examples.source: These include the natively available gates (CX/CZ on IBM devices)
examples.translation: אלה כוללים את ה-Gates הזמינים ילידים (CX/CZ על מכשירי IBM)
examples.suggested: אלה כוללים את ה-Gates הזמינים באופן מקורי (CX/CZ על מכשירי IBM)
examples.why: "ילידים" (natives/indigenous) is a wrong-register literal calque of "natively available"; reads as machine translation, not how a Hebrew engineer would phrase it.

examples.source: provides Qedma-optimized fractional-angle $Rzz(\theta)$ gates
examples.translation: ומספק Gates $Rzz(\theta)$ אופטמיזציה על ידי Qedma
examples.suggested: ומספק Gates $Rzz(\theta)$ עם זוויות שבריות שעברו אופטימיזציה על ידי Qedma
examples.why: The participle is dropped: "Gates Rzz אופטמיזציה על ידי Qedma" is ungrammatical — needs "שעברו אופטימיזציה" (that were optimized). Reads as broken MT.

examples.source: optimized (optimization) — recurring
examples.translation: אופטמיזציה / שאופטמו / אופטמו vs אופטימיזציה
examples.suggested: אופטימיזציה / שעברו אופטימיזציה (consistently)
examples.why: Same concept spelled two ways within the file: clipped "אופטמ-" forms (lines 33, 40) vs standard "אופטימיזציה" (lines 41, 66). Within-file inconsistency.

examples.source: on three connected loops of ibm_kawasaki
examples.translation: על שלושה לולאות מחוברות של ibm_kawasaki
examples.suggested: על שלוש לולאות מחוברות של ibm_kawasaki
examples.why: Gender-agreement error: "לולאה" is feminine, so the numeral must be "שלוש", not masculine "שלושה". Isolated grammar slip, meaning intact.

editor_note: This reads as genuine native technical Italian, not machine translation: pedagogical connective tissue is idiomatic ("non hanno nulla a che fare l'uno con l'altro", "Come suggerisce il nome, questa è effettivamente una base", "Questa matrice unitaria non è niente di speciale, è solo un esempio", "Per essere chiari riguardo alla permutazione dei ket"), and the informal "noi/tu" instructional register holds consistently (possiamo, vediamo, consideriamo, Nota che...) matching the English "we". Core domain terminology is correct and conventional for Italian QC writing and applied consistently within the file: "qubit", "entangled"/"entanglement", "stato prodotto", "prodotto tensoriale", "vettore di stato quantistico", "norma euclidea", "matrice unitaria", "trasposto coniugato", "base standard", "operazione SWAP"/"controlled-X", and "gate di Fredkin/Toffoli" are all handled the way the field actually keeps or translates them. Meaning is faithful throughout — the entanglement argument, the no-faster-than-light caveat (line 354), the normalization analogy, and the reverse/inverse distinction ("inversione"/"inversa", line 813) all land exactly as in the English with no drift, negation flips, or dropped caveats. The only blemishes are cosmetic: the course title "General formulation of quantum information" is capitalized two different ways within the file (sentence case "Formulazione generale dell'informazione quantistica" at line 187 vs title case "Formulazione Generale dell'Informazione Quantistica" at line 539), and "density matrix" appears once as "matrici densità" (line 187) without the preposition versus the standard "matrice di densità" (line 539); neither misleads. Clean PASS.

examples.source: density matrices (which are discussed in the General formulation of quantum information course)
examples.translation: matrici densità (trattate nel corso Formulazione generale dell'informazione quantistica) ... la nozione di matrice di densità ... corso Formulazione Generale dell'Informazione Quantistica
examples.suggested: Use one form consistently: 'matrice di densità' and 'Formulazione generale dell'informazione quantistica' (sentence case) in both places
examples.why: Same term/title rendered two ways within the file — 'matrici densità' vs 'matrice di densità', and the course title in sentence case at L187 but title case at L539. Purely cosmetic consistency, does not change meaning.

examples.source: and conditioned upon the measurement producing this outcome
examples.translation: e condizionatamente al fatto che la misura produca questo risultato
examples.why: Accurate and grammatical, but 'condizionatamente al fatto che' is somewhat heavy; a native writer might prefer 'e, condizionatamente a questo risultato della misura,'. Stylistic nicety only — not a defect.

editor_note: This is competent, faithful technical Korean prose that teaches as clearly as the English, with correct and consistent domain terminology throughout: 해밀토니안 (Hamiltonian), 부분공간 (subspace), 바닥상태 (ground state), 유니터리 크릴로프 공간 / 거듭제곱 크릴로프 공간 (unitary / power Krylov space), 고유값·고유상태 (eigenvalue/eigenstate), 트로터/트로터화 (Trotter/Trotterization), 변분 (variational), and 가환(commuting) Pauli operators are all rendered conventionally and used uniformly. 앤셰츠 (ansatz) and 쇼트 (shots) are kept as defensible consistent transliterations. Two real issues keep it from a clean PASS: (1) register — the file is written entirely in 합니다체 (e.g. 결합합니다, 고려합니다, 설정합니다), not the 해요체 convention the house style asks for; it is internally consistent and standard for technical docs, so it does not mislead, but it is a systematic register deviation rather than an isolated slip. (2) a small precision/naturalness flattening in the Summary: "lattice problems which are amenable to pre-fault tolerant hardware" became "결함 전 하드웨어에 접근 가능한 격자 문제", where "pre-fault tolerant" loses the fault-tolerance sense (reads literally as "before-error") and "amenable to" is softened to "접근 가능한" (accessible to) rather than "suited/tractable". Meaning is otherwise intact across all paragraphs; the only other nit is a calqued "많은 이러한 기법들은" for "Many such techniques". No semantic drift, no terminology errors, no within-file inconsistencies.

examples.source: It consists of a single example… / we consider the Hamiltonian…
examples.translation: 구성되어 있습니다 / 고려합니다 / 설정합니다 (합니다체 throughout)
examples.suggested: Consider 해요체 (구성되어 있어요 / 고려해요 / 설정해요) per the house convention
examples.why: The file is uniformly in formal 합니다체 rather than the requested informal 해요체. Consistent and standard for technical docs, so MINOR, but a systematic register choice rather than a stray slip.

examples.source: it is wise to address lattice problems which are amenable to pre-fault tolerant hardware
examples.translation: 결함 전 하드웨어에 접근 가능한 격자 문제를 다루는 것이 현명합니다
examples.suggested: 사전 결함 허용(pre-fault-tolerant) 하드웨어에 적합한 격자 문제를 다루는 것이 현명합니다
examples.why: 'pre-fault tolerant' is flattened to '결함 전' (literally 'before-error'), losing the fault-TOLERANCE sense; 'amenable to' is softened to '접근 가능한' (accessible) instead of 'suited/tractable'. Mild precision loss, not outright wrong.

examples.source: Many such techniques are available in the Qiskit synthesis module
examples.translation: 많은 이러한 기법들은 [Qiskit 합성 모듈]에서 사용 가능합니다
examples.suggested: 이러한 기법 중 다수는 [Qiskit 합성 모듈]에서 사용할 수 있습니다
examples.why: '많은 이러한 기법들은' is a calque of English 'Many such techniques'; a native technical writer would write '이러한 기법 중 다수는'. Isolated translationese, grammatical but stiff.

editor_note: The bulk of this file is genuinely fluent, native-sounding technical Malay: domain terms are confidently and consistently handled — "berkomut" for "commute" (~9 occurrences), "keadaan tanah" (ground state), "nilai jangkaan" (expectation value), "rentetan Pauli" (Pauli string), "kecerunan" (gradient), and qubit/gate/ansatz/Hamiltonian/Estimator/Sampler/backend sensibly kept in English. Register holds informal "anda" throughout and the pedagogy reads as well as the English. The reason for FAIL is a core-terminology mistranslation in the second "Uji kefahaman anda" answer at line 740: "groups of commuting Pauli operators" is rendered "kumpulan operator Pauli yang bertukar" — "bertukar" means swap/exchange/change, the opposite of "commute", and every other place in the very same file correctly says "berkomut", so it is both wrong and self-inconsistent, and it sits in a teaching answer where the student is being told the criterion is commuting groups. Two additional minor issues: a typo "penumapaiannya" at line 666 (should be "penumpuannya", convergence — correctly spelled elsewhere), and "barren plateaus" rendered three ways ("dataran tandus" L281, "Tanah tandus" L667 heading, "kawasan tandus" L667 body).

examples.source: sorted into relatively few groups of commuting Pauli operators
examples.translation: boleh disusun ke dalam kumpulan operator Pauli yang bertukar yang agak sedikit
examples.suggested: boleh disusun ke dalam kumpulan operator Pauli berkomut yang agak sedikit
examples.why: "bertukar" means swap/exchange, the opposite of "commute". The correct domain term "berkomut" is used correctly ~9 times elsewhere in this same file, so this is both a mistranslation of a core term and a within-file inconsistency in a check-your-understanding answer that could mislead a student.

examples.source: steer it away from the true minimum or delay its convergence
examples.translation: menjauhkannya dari minimum sebenar atau melambatkan penumapaiannya
examples.suggested: menjauhkannya dari minimum sebenar atau melambatkan penumpuannya
examples.why: "penumapaiannya" is a typo; the intended word "penumpuan" (convergence) is spelled correctly at lines 448 and 663. Isolated spelling slip, not misleading.

examples.source: barren plateaus
examples.translation: "dataran tandus" (L281) vs "Tanah tandus / kawasan tandus" (L667)
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering, e.g. "dataran tandus", and use it consistently
examples.why: The same named concept is rendered three ways across the file. The English gloss at L667 limits reader confusion, so this is a minor consistency slip rather than a meaning error.

editor_note: The body prose is genuinely native-quality and faithful: paragraphs like line 78 ("Instrukcja `Move` jest koncepcyjnie równoważna operacji reset... częściowe zapadnięcie stanu reszty systemu") and line 171 ("z łącznego rozkładu kwaziprawdopodobieństwa zdekomponowanych bramek") read like a Polish quantum engineer wrote them, with correct domain terms ("wartości oczekiwane", "obserwowalna", "narzut próbkowania", "kwaziprawdopodobieństwo", "splątany", "poddoświadczeń") and consistent informal "ty" (znajdziesz, Zwróć uwagę). The FAIL is driven by a pervasive within-file inconsistency for the word "circuit": it is correctly translated as "obwód" in running text (lines 14, 31, 78, 117, 155) but left as untranslated, capitalized English "Circuit" in six headings/bullets (lines 21 "Cięcie Circuit i obserwowalnej", 29 "Tworzenie Circuit do cięcia", 76, 107, 115), and the same defect hits "backend" (heading line 181 "Wybór Backend" undeclined vs. body "backendu/backendach"). These look like un-restored placeholder tokens, not a native writer's choice, and exceed the ≥3-inconsistency threshold. A secondary minor inconsistency: "qubit" is rendered with Polish "k" everywhere (kubit, kubitów, siedmiokubitowego) yet the compound keeps the English "q" in "dwuqubitowa/dwuqubitowych" (lines 2, 78) instead of the conventional "dwukubitowy".

examples.source: Cut the circuit and observable. / Create a circuit to cut
examples.translation: Cięcie Circuit i obserwowalnej. / Tworzenie Circuit do cięcia
examples.suggested: Cięcie obwodu i obserwowalnej. / Tworzenie obwodu do cięcia
examples.why: "circuit" is correctly translated as "obwód" in the body but left as untranslated, capitalized English "Circuit" in six headings/bullets — same noun rendered two ways 6+ times, reading as an un-restored placeholder rather than native prose.

examples.source: Choose a backend
examples.translation: Wybór Backend
examples.suggested: Wybór backendu
examples.why: Heading leaves "Backend" as undeclined English, while the body uses properly declined Polish ("backendu", "backendach"). Inconsistent and grammatically stranded in the heading.

examples.source: two-qubit / two-qubit Move instructions
examples.translation: dwuqubitowa / dwuqubitowych
examples.suggested: dwukubitowa / dwukubitowych
examples.why: "qubit" is rendered with Polish "k" everywhere else (kubit, siedmiokubitowego, czterokubitowe), but the compound retains the English "q". The conventional Polish form is "dwukubitowy".

editor_note: This is native-quality technical Portuguese throughout a long, math-heavy KQD lesson. The prose reads as written by a Lusophone quantum engineer, not as MT: idiomatic constructions like "Vamos nos convencer de que...", "É aqui finalmente que o computador quântico entra em cena", "Vale a pena analisar isso com cuidado" and "Fique à vontade para pular a avaliação" are fluent and uncalqued, and the informal "você" register holds consistently ("se você executar o cálculo novamente", "a menos que você queira entender"). Domain terminology is correct and consistent: autovalores/autovetores/autoestados, "Hamiltoniano", "subespaço de Krylov", "valores esperados" (expectation values), "estado fundamental" (ground state), "norma-A", "descida mais íngreme" (steepest descent), with qubit/shots/backend/Estimator/transpilar kept per Qiskit convention. I read the at-risk passages (the dt-choice caveat at L946, the reworded Krylov-dimension paragraph at L976, the generalized-eigenvalue explanation at L776, and the Appendix upper/lower-bound proofs) for meaning and found no semantic drift, no inverted inequalities, and no softened/strengthened claims. The only blemishes are trivial: a within-file inconsistency where Pauli strings are called "cadeia de Pauli" at L751 but "strings de Pauli" at L1668/L1671 (both acceptable, but mixed), and an untranslated English ordinal where "the $i^\text{th}$ spin" became "o $i^\text{th}$ spin" at L906 instead of "$i$-ésimo" (a cosmetic leak inside math, not prose). Neither misleads; this is essentially a clean, faithful, well-taught translation.

examples.source: a set of such Pauli strings that commute / many non-commuting Pauli strings
examples.translation: cadeia de Pauli (L751) vs strings de Pauli (L1668, L1671)
examples.why: Same concept (Pauli string) rendered two ways within one file; both are valid PT-BR but the mixing is a minor consistency slip.
examples.suggested: Pick one rendering (e.g. 'string de Pauli') and use it throughout.

examples.source: the $i^\text{th}$ spin can be influenced by its nearest neighbors
examples.translation: o $i^\text{th}$ spin pode ser influenciado pelos seus vizinhos mais próximos
examples.why: The English ordinal suffix 'th' was left untranslated inside the prose/math; a native writer would localize the ordinal.
examples.suggested: o $i$-ésimo spin (and likewise $(i-1)$-ésimo / $(i+1)$-ésimo).

editor_note: This reads as genuine, fluent Romanian technical prose written by someone who understands the physics, not as raw MT — sentences like "măsurarea prăbușește starea cuantică", "stare proprie a lui X", and the eavesdropping narration flow naturally and teach with the same intuition as the English. Core terminology is correct and overwhelmingly consistent: "qubit/qubiți", "stare proprie" (eigenstate), "bază/baze" (basis), "fidelitate", "zgomot" (noise), "măsurătoare", "backend"/"primitive Runtime" left in English appropriately, and the informal "tu" register holds throughout (Consideră, Reține, hai să). The one real defect is a within-file terminology split for "one-time pad": it is rendered "pad de unică folosință" everywhere in the body (lines 88–156, 451, 505) but switches to "bloc de unică folosință" in the closing Questions section (lines 945, 953, 957) — the same concept named two ways. Smaller slips: "încâlciți/încâlcire" for entangled/entanglement is a defensible-but-unusual literal choice (used consistently), and "fie oricare stare proprie" (line 601, for "either eigenstate") has a redundant "fie" that a native editor would trim. None of these mislead or distort meaning; they are isolated polish items, so MINOR_ISSUES rather than FAIL.

examples.source: This is called a "one-time pad". / An encryption/decryption key (a one-time pad)
examples.translation: Aceasta se numește „pad de unică folosință" ... / O cheie de criptare/decriptare (un bloc de unică folosință)
examples.suggested: Use one rendering throughout — keep „pad de unică folosință" (already used 8+ times) in the Critical concepts/Questions block instead of switching to „bloc de unică folosință".
examples.why: Same domain term rendered two different ways within one file; a consistent convention is expected in teaching material so the student maps one Romanian phrase to one concept.

examples.source: or she could assume she chose the basis incorrectly and choose either eigenstate of the opposite basis
examples.translation: sau ar putea presupune că a ales baza greșit și să aleagă fie oricare stare proprie a bazei opuse
examples.suggested: ...și să aleagă oricare dintre stările proprii ale bazei opuse
examples.why: The stray „fie" before „oricare" is redundant in Romanian and reads slightly machine-like; a native technical writer would drop it.

examples.source: share entangled qubits / the many entangled qubits cannot be simulated
examples.translation: partaja qubiți încâlciți / mulți qubiți încâlciți nu pot fi simulați
examples.suggested: Acceptable as-is if kept consistent; „întrețesuți"/„împletiți" or leaving "entanglement" in English are the more common field conventions.
examples.why: „încâlcit" (tangled/snarled) is a defensible but non-standard rendering of entangled; flagged only as a borderline terminology choice, not an error — it is used consistently.

editor_note: This reads as genuine, well-edited technical Tagalog written by someone fluent in the quantum/dev domain, not raw MT. The translation correctly follows the conventional Tagalog tech-writing pattern of keeping established English terms (quantum computer, circuit, kernel, backend, Simulator Mode, AerSimulator, FakeBackends, transpilation, error mitigation, credential injection, code execution, Bell state) untranslated while genuinely translating the connective and explanatory prose (kurso/course, gabay/guide, nilalaman/content, materyales/materials, napapanahon/up-to-date, nakaayos/structured) — all used consistently within the file with no inconsistencies. The casual register holds throughout (ka/iyong/mo, "Tumalon na sa isang tutorial o gabay" for "Jump into a tutorial or guide"), and meaning is faithful: quantities (42/171/154/14, 40+, ~3 GB, ~60 MB) all match, and conditions are preserved ("ngunit ang kanilang web application ay hindi" = "but their web application is not"; "maaaring may ilang hindi pa nasa doQumentation" = "some may not yet be in doQumentation"). Only two cosmetic blemishes keep this from flawless: "dokumentasyon library" (line 15) awkwardly pairs a translated noun with an English head noun, and "Habang ikaw ay umuusad" (line 65) uses a mildly formal "ay"-inversion a touch stiffer than the surrounding casual tone — neither misleads nor flattens the pedagogy.

examples.source: ...open-source frontend to IBM Quantum's complete open-source tutorials, courses, and documentation library.
examples.translation: ...open-source na frontend sa kumpletong open-source na mga tutorial, kurso, at dokumentasyon library ng IBM Quantum.
examples.suggested: ...mga tutorial, kurso, at library ng dokumentasyon ng IBM Quantum.
examples.why: "dokumentasyon library" pairs a Tagalogized noun with an English head noun, reading slightly machine-like; reordering to "library ng dokumentasyon" is more natural. Cosmetic, not misleading.

examples.source: As you progress, head to IBM's official platform:
examples.translation: Habang ikaw ay umuusad, pumunta sa opisyal na platform ng IBM:
examples.suggested: Habang umuusad ka, pumunta sa opisyal na platform ng IBM:
examples.why: The "ikaw ay" inversion is grammatically fine but slightly more formal/literary than the casual tone used elsewhere; "umuusad ka" matches the file's informal register better.

editor_note: This reads as native, well-edited Tagalog technical prose written in the consistent informal register the course uses ("tayo/natin/Narito"), not raw MT. Domain terminology is handled by the correct convention of keeping core terms in English — qubit, gate, circuit, query gate, phase kickback, layer, Hadamard gate, string, function, two-to-one/one-to-one, null space, Gaussian elimination, modulo — while translating genuinely translatable concepts consistently: "sukat" for measurement (lines 105/193/217), "estado" for state, "probabilidad" for probability, "pagsusuri" for analysis. Meaning is faithful throughout: negations, quantities ($2^{n-1}$, exactly-two, 99.9%, $1-2^{-r}$), the Case 1/Case 2 structure, and the pedagogical intuitions (the "bucket" splitting at line 143, the hidden-string XOR caveat at 302) all match the English with equal clarity. Only two isolated slips, neither misleading: "mas kaunting $2^{n/2-1}-1$" (lines 296, 303) renders "fewer than X" in a slightly stiff form where "mas kaunti sa X" reads more idiomatically, and "tunay o kumplikadong numero" (line 287) is a literal over-translation of "real or complex numbers" where the field usually keeps "complex numbers" in English.

examples.source: that algorithm makes fewer than $2^{n/2 - 1} - 1$ queries
examples.translation: ang algorithm na iyon ay gumagawa ng mas kaunting $2^{n/2 - 1} - 1$ na query
examples.suggested: ...ay gumagawa ng mas kaunti sa $2^{n/2 - 1} - 1$ na query
examples.why: 'mas kaunting X' for 'fewer than X' is a mild calque; 'mas kaunti sa X' is the idiomatic Tagalog comparative. Meaning is fully preserved, so this is a polish-level slip only (recurs at line 303).

examples.source: as it does with real or complex numbers
examples.translation: tulad ng sa mga tunay o kumplikadong numero
examples.suggested: tulad ng sa mga real o complex na numero
examples.why: 'tunay' (real) and 'kumplikado' (complex) literally translate the number-type names, but the field conventionally keeps 'complex numbers' in English. Not misleading and defensible, hence MINOR not FAIL.

editor_note: This reads like genuine native Ukrainian technical prose, not machine translation: idiomatic connectors ("По мірі того як атоми зближуються", "заощаджуючи квантові ресурси завдяки симетрії") and naturally-rendered teaching analogies (LEGO "деталей", radio "ручок налаштування на радіоприймачі", climbing "із заплющеними очима"), with the informal "ти" register held consistently throughout. Core domain terminology is correct and confident — кубіт, гейт, квантова схема, спостережуваний, анзац, збіжність, суперпозиція/заплутаність, сфера Блоха, оператори Паулі, бекенд — and meaning is faithful (the ≥E_0 inequality, the ring→fuller→even Bloch-coverage progression, the 10^48 bits vs 10^24 stars, and the "energy is released" sign of the reaction are all preserved accurately). The only blemishes are isolated within-file inconsistencies that do not mislead: "Hamiltonian" is capitalized "Гамільтоніан" in the body but lowercase "гамільтоніан" throughout the Review section (L1016–1029); the unit Hartree is transliterated "Гартрі" once (L338) but "Хартрі" everywhere else; "expectation value" is rendered both "очікуване значення" (L363, 1020) and "математичне сподівання" (L585, 757); and "target hardware" is given the colloquial "залізо" (L549, 583, 747) where the file itself elsewhere uses the neutral "апаратне забезпечення" (L313). None of these change meaning — they are register/consistency polish, hence MINOR not FAIL.

examples.source: Hamiltonian (capitalization)
examples.translation: Body: "оператор Гамільтоніана", "молекулярний Гамільтоніан"; Review: "роль гамільтоніана у VQE", "енергію основного стану гамільтоніана"
examples.suggested: гамільтоніан (lowercase, per Ukrainian orthography for eponymous operators)
examples.why: Same term capitalized in the lesson body but lowercased throughout the Review/True-False/MCQ block — an internal inconsistency for one concept.

examples.source: Hartree (unit)
examples.suggested: Pick one transliteration (Хартрі is the dominant form here)
examples.why: The same physical unit is transliterated two different ways within one file.

examples.source: expectation value of the Hamiltonian
examples.translation: "математичне сподівання Гамільтоніана" (L585, 757) vs "очікуване значення енергії" (L363) / "очікуване значення" (L1020, 1025)
examples.suggested: Use one rendering for the same concept consistently
examples.why: Both are valid Ukrainian, but the file alternates between them for the identical notion, which can read as two distinct ideas to a learner.

examples.source: Optimize for target hardware / Execute on target hardware
examples.translation: "Оптимізація під цільове залізо" / "Виконання на цільовому залізі"
examples.suggested: апаратне забезпечення / обладнання
examples.why: "залізо" is colloquial slang for hardware; the same file uses neutral "апаратне забезпечення" at L313, so the teaching-register headings would read more consistently with that form.
